ip_outcome_note: Card name is 'Rowlet'; depicted figure (small round owl creature with a leafy bow-tie, perched on a branch, brown-white plumage) matches the canonical Grass/Flying Alola starter, National Pokedex no. 722. Verified via card name, PokemonTCG.io metadata, Bulbapedia: Rowlet (Pokemon), and Serebii Pokedex 722.

trivia_bullets.text: Rowlet is National Pokedex no. 722, one of the three First Partner Pokemon of Alola in Pokemon Sun and Moon (Gen 7, 2016). It is a Grass/Flying-type and holds the highest base HP of all unevolved starter Pokemon. [Serebii.net Pokedex 722; Bulbapedia: Rowlet; via WebSearch]

Rowlet's design is based on the barn owl (Tyto alba), introduced to Hawaii. Its Grass typing may also reference the pueo (Asio flammeus sandwichensis), a short-eared owl native only to the Hawaiian archipelago that flies over grassy areas — a double-coded nod to Alola's Hawaiian setting. [Bulbapedia: Rowlet design section; via WebSearch]

Flavor text on this card: "It sends its feathers, which are as sharp as blades, flying in attack. Its legs are strong, so its kicks are also formidable." Established at base form; the feather-blade motif fully realizes in the evolution line, where Decidueye plucks arrow quills from its wings and fires them in a tenth of a second. [PokemonTCG.io: sm12-17 flavorText; frontmatter capture; WebSearch re: Decidueye mechanics]

Rowlet is the only starter whose secondary typing does not carry through its full evolutionary line: Rowlet (Grass/Flying) loses the Flying type at Decidueye (Grass/Ghost in Alola, Grass/Fighting in Hisui). This makes it typologically distinct from all other starter lines across both its regional forms. [Bulbapedia: Rowlet / Decidueye; via WebSearch]
